rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHARLES YOUNG CHONG et al TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions To Trustee Aaron Chong; Compel Trustee To Provide Information Render Proper Accounting, And Distribute Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/11/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. Notice must be given directly to an interested party, not just to the attorney representing the interested party. [Prob. C. Sect. 1214 and CRC 7.51.] Examiners will not review copies of unfiled papers. Courtesy copies must be file stamped copies. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (i.e., a verified supplement providing the names and last known addresses of the vested or contingent beneficiaries and proof of service of notice to all beneficiaries or waivers of notice signed by them) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/2/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

A copy of a supplement or other papers addressing the issues must be served on the person who requested special notice. A proof of service must be filed. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH ROBERT PICCININI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/11/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. [Probate Code Section 9202(a).] Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. Paragraph 17 of the petition states that the executor took no actions under her IAEA authority, but she clearly must have sold the bulk of the securities as there is now just cash and the Save Mart shares on hand. Attorney costs must be itemized, showing the date, the payee, the description, and the amount of each incurred cost, not just lump sums. (For example, the filing fee for this petition was $435, not $465, and there was no filing fee of $1,545.) The total also appears to be incorrect. The prayer does not specifically include a request that attorney costs be reimbursed. A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary, not just a percentage or a fraction. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] What "unpaid costs of administration" does the executor estimate to be approximately $30,000? [LRSF 14.26 I.3.] (The previously authorized partial compensation amount noted in paragraphs 28 and 29 of the petition is incorrect. The attorney presumably waives any statutory fee in excess of $10,000,000..?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (likely, an amendment to the petition) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/2/21. If an amendment is filed, notice of the amendment is required. [CRC 7.53(a).] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIA EMME FITCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the full disclosure regarding Probate Code Section 9202(b) (not 9202(a)) was not made regarding any PREVIOUS incarceration of an heir, and the sole surviving parent is the only person entitled to distribution pursuant to Probate Code Section 6402(b) as the father predeceased. Attorney costs also appear to have been miscalculated. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/28/2021. 1) Please provide the legal basis for filing this petition using a power of attorney and address Probate Code 13650. 2) Please explain what appears to be a signature by mark on the power of attorney. 3) Petitioner must file an attachment 7. Attachment 7 is different from attachment 7a and 7b. (See petition page 1 #7) 4) Please address Local Rules 14.26 A. and 14.26 B. 5) Petitioner must give notice of the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/12/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN QUINCY ADAMS JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/28/2021. Petitioner may want to seek the assistance of an attorney. 1) Please explain why a petition for probate has been filed in there are no assets. 2) Petitioner must check a box on the petition page 3 under #5(a) (3) or (4) and (5) or (6) and (7) or (8). 3) Petitioner must state the relationship to Decedent and address of Lester Lewis and Jessina Adams on page 4 under #8. 4) Petitioner must be listed on page 4 under #8 including the name and relationship to decedent, age, and address. 5) All heirs must be listed on page 4 under #8. The date of death must be specified for all deceased heirs. [LRSF 14.18] 6) Please provide the legal basis for filing a notice of proposed action when Ivery Hammon Jr has not been appointed. 7) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 8) Petitioner must file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). 9) Petitioner must file Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). 10) Petitioner must file proof of publication. 11) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order (DE-140) to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/12/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
